trip.name: Maine Coast
trip.title: Maine Coast Jernie
trip.tagline: Portland → Bar Harbor & Acadia → Southwest Harbor

stops.summary: Your introduction to Maine — two and a half days to eat your way through the best food city in New England. Land, drop bags, and get straight to it. Portland Head Light and the Old Port on day one, the Lucky Catch lobster cruise on day two, then north on Sunday morning. Don’t skip Eventide or Mr. Tuna. The lobster cruise is the highlight — gear up for it.

Three days in Acadia — the physical center of this trip. Hike hard, eat well, and get on the water. The Beehive is yours if you want it; the Jordan Pond Loop works for everyone. Cadillac at sunrise on Wednesday morning requires a Recreation.gov reservation set two days prior at 10am EST. Book Havana the moment you read this if you haven’t.

The decompression leg — quieter, slower, and just as good. Southwest Harbor is the local side of MDI: no tour buses, real Maine character. The Claremont is exceptional. Hike Acadia Mountain or Beech Mountain in the afternoon, Bass Harbor Lighthouse at golden hour, dinner at Little Fern. Flight out of Bangor on Friday — Stacy’s group leaves by 10:30am, you’re on the 2:22pm.

bookings.label: Courtyard Downtown/Waterfront
bookings.note: Modern Marriott property on Commercial Street — you’re steps from the Old Port, the harbor, and every restaurant worth going to. Parking on-site, which matters in Portland. Use it as a base and spend all your time outside it.

bookings.label: Canopy by Hilton Portland Waterfront
bookings.note: Hilton’s lifestyle brand — newer, design-forward, with a rooftop bar and a location right on the water. More boutique feel than the standard Courtyard. Walking distance to everything.

bookings.label: West Street Hotel — King Ocean View
bookings.note: High floor ocean view room — Frenchman Bay spread out below you. Heated rooftop pool open year-round, boutique property, five-minute walk to everything in town. Bar Harbor Club access included: hot tub, spa, and fitness center.

bookings.label: Bar Harbor Inn
bookings.note: Classic Bar Harbor grande dame — oceanfront property with wraparound water views, private beach access, and the Reading Room restaurant on-site. Built in 1887, impeccably maintained. Steps to everything downtown.

bookings.label: Rental Car Drop-Off — Avis at BGR
lines: 📍 287 Godfrey Blvd, Bangor, ME 04401 (Bangor Intl Airport)
⏰ Allow ~30 min from The Claremont — plan checkout accordingly.

bookings.label: The Claremont Hotel — Phillips House King w/ Deck
bookings.note: Historic 1883 Victorian inn, fully renovated 2021. Phillips House room features a gas fireplace and a private deck with partial Somes Sound views. Botanica Spa on-site, private dock, and the best restaurant on the quiet side of the island downstairs. Book the spa before you leave home.

bookings.label: The Claremont Hotel — Ocean View 1BR Cottage
bookings.note: Private 1BR cottage on the Claremont grounds, directly on Somes Sound. Full kitchen, water-facing deck, complete separation from the main inn. Quiet, secluded, and exceptional — the best accommodation on this leg by a wide margin.

itinerary_items.text: Land at PWM · pick up Avis Full-Size SUV at the jetport
Check in at Courtyard Downtown/Waterfront — drop bags, freshen up, get oriented
Portland Head Light · 15 min drive to Cape Elizabeth · most photographed lighthouse in New England · go while it’s not crowded yet
Old Port + Exchange Street · wander, browse, hit Ports of Call & Shipwreck & Cargo for souvenirs · grab Holy Donuts on the way back
Dinner at Eventide Oyster Co. or Mr. Tuna · go early to beat the wait
Breakfast at Tandem Coffee + Bakery · best breakfast sandwich in Portland · get there before the morning rush
Spring Point Ledge Lighthouse or Peaks Island Ferry + golf cart · pick one for the morning · both are easy and fun
Lucky Catch Lobstering Cruise · 90 min on the water hauling traps · Portland Lobster Co. cooks your catch right after
Harbor Fish Market · waterfront institution since 1966 · worth walking through even if you don’t buy · pure, uncut Portland
Holy Donuts + Coastal Creamery · afternoon snack lap through the Old Port · non-negotiable
Dinner at Scales · splurge night on the wharf · reserve ahead or you won’t get in
Breakfast at Becky’s Diner · local institution · great chowder · no fuss · fuel up before the drive
Check out · load the SUV · ~2.5 hr drive to Bar Harbor via US-1
McLoon’s Lobster · Spruce Head, Rockland area · legendary lobster shack on the water · worth the slight detour off US-1 through Thomaston/Rockland · call ahead
Stop: Glisten Oyster Farm · Damariscotta, ME (right on the US-1 route) · call ahead to confirm hours
Arrive Bar Harbor · check in West Street Hotel + Bar Harbor Inn · drop bags
Agamont Park walk · harbor stroll · get your bearings before dinner · Bar Island low-tide crossing if tides align · check the chart before going
Dinner at Havana · best restaurant in Bar Harbor · will be full Memorial Day weekend
Arrive from Portland · check in West Street Hotel & Bar Harbor Inn · decompress from the drive
Agamont Park walk · easy waterfront stroll · get the lay of the land · perfect first activity with Ford
Bar Island crossing if tides allow — a land bridge appears at low tide only · check the chart before going · it’s worth it
Dinner at Havana · best restaurant in Bar Harbor · book before the trip — Memorial Day weekend fills it completely
Breakfast at Jeannie’s Great Maine Breakfast · buttermilk blueberry pancakes · go early before it fills up
Ocean Path Trail · easy 4-mile round trip along dramatic rocky shoreline · passes Thunder Hole, Sand Beach, Otter Cliff · perfect for the full group
Thunder Hole · best when ocean swells hit 1–2 hours before high tide · check the chart before you go · it’s spectacular when timed right
Lunch at Peekytoe Provisions · freshest crab and lobster on the island · great after a morning walk
Free time · Sand Beach · Ben & Bill’s ice cream · Sherman’s Books · let Ford dictate the pace
Memorial Day fireworks on Bar Harbor waterfront · check local schedule for exact time · Agamont Park is the spot to watch from

Beehive Trail — Jeremy & Jennie only (iron rungs bolted into cliff faces, exposed ledges, serious scramble). Stacy/Justin/Ford: Jordan Pond Loop instead (3.5 mi, flat, stunning glacial pond). Start early to beat the heat and crowds.
Jordan Pond House Popovers · non-negotiable post-hike tradition · meet here as a group · famous popovers with jam and tea on the lawn
Thuya Garden · 20 min drive to Northeast Harbor · hidden formal hillside gardens · a quiet gem most tourists miss · especially beautiful in late May bloom
Sunset Sail from Bar Harbor pier · Margaret Todd tall ship or similar schooner · 2-hour sail into Frenchman Bay at golden hour · book a few days ahead
Dinner at The Reading Room (Bar Harbor Inn) · panoramic harbor views from nearly every seat · one splurge dinner for the setting alone

Cadillac Mountain Sunrise · first sunrise in the contiguous US in late spring · summit road requires Recreation.gov reservation 2 days prior at 10am EST · OR hike up on foot — no reservation needed for hikers · alarm set for 3:15am
Breakfast at Sunrise Cafe after you descend · best harbor views in Bar Harbor at breakfast · you’ve earned it
Schoodic Peninsula drive · 45 min · dramatic pink granite shoreline · raw, beautiful, far fewer crowds than the main island
Last lunch in Bar Harbor · Side Street Cafe lobster roll · then check out and pack up
Drive to Southwest Harbor (~30 min) · check in at The Claremont · settle in · take in the Somes Sound view from your deck
Dinner at Little Fern at The Claremont · best restaurant on this side of the island · reserve before you leave home
Sleep in. Breakfast on your deck overlooking Somes Sound. This is the decompression leg of the trip — slow down and actually mean it.
Botanica Spa at The Claremont · book a massage before you arrive · after three days of Acadia hiking, your body will demand it · walk-ins are unlikely during Memorial Day weekend
Lunch at Batson River Fish Camp · lobster rolls on The Claremont’s private dock · cold drinks, water right there · perfect
Acadia Mountain Trail (2.5 mi RT, steep, outstanding Somes Sound views from the top) or Beech Mountain + Fire Tower for 360° views — pick one, both are great
Bass Harbor Head Lighthouse · 15 min drive · one of the most photographed shots on the East Coast · arrive early to claim a spot on the rocks below
Pre-dinner drinks at Harry’s Bar at The Claremont · classic cocktails · the right way to end every day here
Dinner at Little Fern at The Claremont · panoramic Somes Sound views · fireplace · reserve before you leave home
Final breakfast · last coffee on the deck · pack and say a proper goodbye to Somes Sound
Check out of The Claremont
Drive to Bangor Airport · ~30 min · return Avis SUV at BGR · Stacy/Justin/Ford depart earlier for their 12:15 PM flight
DL 5254 departs BGR → LGA — Stacy, Justin & Ford (CONFIRMED)
AA 1463 departs BGR → CLT — Jeremy & Jennie (CONFIRMED)
Land in Charlotte · trip complete · go eat a mediocre dinner and miss Maine immediately 🦞

places.name: Eventide Oyster Co.
places.note: Don’t skip the brown butter lobster roll on a steamed bun. Expect a wait — go early or late.

places.name: Scales
places.note: On the wharf with lobstermen docking outside the window. Splurge dinner of your Portland stay. Order the steamed lobster.

places.name: Mr. Tuna
places.note: Food & Wine’s #6 Best Restaurant in the Country (2025). Built around sustainable Gulf of Maine seafood. Get the Tuna de Tigre.

places.name: Luke’s Lobster
places.note: The original Portland lobster shack, born right here on the pier. Clean, sweet claw-and-knuckle on a butter-toasted roll. Fast, affordable, and excellent for lunch.

places.name: Tandem Coffee + Bakery
places.note: Best breakfast sandwich in Portland. Counter service, communal tables. Get there before the morning rush. Their coffee program is serious — don’t just grab and go.

places.name: Holy Donuts
places.note: Portland-famous potato donuts in rotating flavors. Dark chocolate sea salt and maple bacon are the moves. There will be a line. Worth every second.

places.name: Via Vecchia
places.note: Old-school Italian in a warm, candlelit Portland setting. Handmade pasta, wood-fired preparations. Perfect for a night when you need a break from seafood.

places.name: Cliff House Maine
places.note: Located in York, ME — 30 min south of Portland, near Kennebunkport. Cliffside views, fire pits, exceptional dinner. Worth it if you want a splurge night on the Portland end of the trip.

places.name: Street & Co.
places.note: Candle-lit brick-and-beam room, unfussy fresh seafood. Jonah crab sauté and sole Francaise are standouts.

places.name: Becky’s Diner
places.note: Local institution. Go before you drive north. Great chowder, no fuss.

places.name: Bard Coffee
places.note: Specialty coffee in the Old Port. Single-origin pour-overs and excellent espresso. The right way to start any Portland morning.

places.name: Coastal Creamery
places.note: Local scoop shop in the Old Port. The correct way to end an afternoon walk. Great flavors built around Maine ingredients.

places.name: Harbor Fish Market
places.note: Old-school waterfront fish market, open since 1966. Walk through for the full experience even if you don’t buy anything. Pure, uncut Portland.

places.name: Portland Head Light
places.note: Most photographed lighthouse in New England. Fort Williams Park in Cape Elizabeth — 15 min from downtown. Rocky shoreline, dramatic drops to the sea. Go before golden hour for the best light.

places.name: Old Port + Exchange Street
places.note: Cobblestone waterfront district — wander, browse, eat. The heart of the city. Hit Ports of Call, Shipwreck & Cargo, and Soleil for souvenirs.

places.name: Spring Point Ledge Lighthouse
places.note: Walk the granite causeway right up to the lighthouse. Great harbor views back to Portland. Combine with the Spring Point Shoreway trail for a longer loop.

places.name: Peaks Island Ferry + Golf Cart
places.note: 17-minute Casco Bay Lines ferry to a car-free island. Rent a golf cart and circle the island at your own pace. Surreal, quiet, gorgeous views back to the Portland skyline.

places.name: Eastern Promenade Trail
places.note: Scenic harbor overlook trail. Great for a morning walk before driving north.

places.name: Portland Fire Engine Co. City Tour
places.note: Hop-on tour of Portland in a vintage fire engine. Touristy but genuinely fun — especially with Ford along for the ride.

places.name: Kennebunkport Day Trip
places.note: 30 min south on US-1. Beautiful coastal village, Dock Square shopping, Walker’s Point (Bush compound) visible from the water. Worth a half day if the schedule allows.

places.name: Havana
places.note: 2025 James Beard Award semifinalist. Best restaurant in Bar Harbor by a mile. Book before the trip — it will be full Memorial Day weekend. Get the seafood paella and lobster moqueca.

places.name: Peekytoe Provisions
places.note: Best crab and lobster on the island. Seafood brought in fresh daily. Great for lunch after hiking.

places.name: Jeannie’s Great Maine Breakfast
places.note: Buttermilk blueberry pancakes so loaded with berries the edges turn deep blue. Go early — it fills up fast.

places.name: The Reading Room
places.note: Historic dining room inside the Bar Harbor Inn. Panoramic harbor views from nearly every seat. One splurge dinner for the setting alone — dress up slightly.

places.name: Sweet Pea’s Farm Kitchen
places.note: Tucked inside a working garden in full bloom. Farm-to-table breakfast and lunch with produce coming out of the ground that morning. Go with daylight — the garden is the whole point.

places.name: McLoon’s Lobster
places.note: NOT in Bar Harbor — this is in Spruce Head near Rockland, ~1.5 hrs south. It’s an incredible stop on the Portland → Bar Harbor drive if you route through Thomaston/Rockland. Plan it as a road-trip lunch, not a BH dinner.
places.flag: Near Rockland · stop on drive from Portland

places.name: McKay’s Public House
places.note: Cozy Bar Harbor gastropub. Good burgers and local seafood in an unfussy setting. Great for a lower-key dinner night.

places.name: Sunrise Cafe
places.note: Best breakfast views in Bar Harbor. Eggs, pancakes, harbor out every window. Perfect after an early Cadillac Mountain sunrise.

places.name: Abel’s on the Water
places.note: Casual waterfront dining on Eagle Lake, ~20 min from town. Fresh lobster and fish on the water. A quieter alternative to downtown.

places.name: Side Street Cafe
places.note: Local go-to for a classic lobster roll at a fair price. Patio seating, no pretense.

places.name: Ben & Bill’s Chocolate Emporium
places.note: Bar Harbor institution. Famous for outrageous flavors including the polarizing lobster ice cream. A rite of passage whether you finish the cone or not.

places.name: Rose Eden Lobster
places.note: Opens Memorial Day weekend. Grab lobster to-go and eat on the harbor. The quintessential Maine moment.

places.name: Beehive Trail ⭐
places.note: Best hike in the park. Iron rungs bolted into cliff faces, exposed ledges, ocean views. Jeremy & Jennie only — not suitable for everyone. Athletic and dramatic.

places.name: Cadillac Mountain Sunrise 🌅
places.note: Cadillac is the first place in the contiguous US to see sunrise in late spring. Summit road reservation required via Recreation.gov 2 days prior at 10am EST — or hike up on foot, no reservation needed.

places.name: Jordan Pond Loop + Popovers
places.note: Stunning glacial pond surrounded by peaks. 3.5 miles, flat and easy. The Jordan Pond House serves famous popovers with jam and tea right after — don’t skip it.

places.name: Ship Harbor Trail
places.note: Easy 1.3-mile loop through spruce forest to a rocky shoreline. Great short walk on the quieter western side of the island — no crowds.

places.name: Sunset Sail
places.note: Several schooner options depart from the Bar Harbor pier. The Margaret Todd tall ship is the classic — 2-hour sail into Frenchman Bay at golden hour. Book a few days ahead.

places.name: Thunder Hole
places.note: Carved sea cave along Ocean Path. Ocean swells compress into the cave and blast upward — go 1–2 hours before high tide for maximum drama. Check tide charts before you go.

places.name: Bar Island at Low Tide
places.note: A land bridge connects downtown to Bar Island at low tide only. Walk across, hike up for harbor views, get back before the tide covers the crossing. Check charts — the window is short.

places.name: Ocean Path Trail
places.note: Easy 4-mile round trip along dramatic rocky shoreline. Passes Thunder Hole, Sand Beach, and Otter Cliff. Perfect for the full group — flat, manageable with Ford.

places.name: Agamont Park
places.note: Small but beautiful waterfront park in downtown Bar Harbor. Easy harbor stroll, great views. Good first walk after you arrive to get oriented.

places.name: Schoodic Peninsula
places.note: Underrated section of Acadia, 45 min from Bar Harbor. Pink granite shoreline meets open Atlantic. Far fewer crowds than the main island — dramatic, raw, and worth every minute of the drive.

places.name: Thuya Garden
places.note: Hidden formal garden in Northeast Harbor, 20 min from Bar Harbor. Immaculate hillside gardens above a serene pond. A quiet gem most tourists completely miss — especially beautiful in late May bloom.

places.name: Sherman’s Books & Stationery
places.note: Best bookstore in Bar Harbor. Excellent local Maine section, great gifts. Hard to leave empty-handed.

places.name: The Annex
places.note: Bar with live music most nights in season. Good spot to end any evening — especially after a long hike day when dinner is done and no one wants to sleep yet.

places.name: Little Fern at The Claremont
places.note: Best restaurant on this side of the island. Panoramic Somes Sound views, fireplace, exceptional food. Reserve before you leave home — don’t try to walk in.

places.name: Batson River Fish Camp
places.note: Right on The Claremont’s private dock. Lobster rolls, cold drinks, on the water. Perfect lunch every day you’re here.

places.name: Harry’s Bar at The Claremont
places.note: Where the evening starts. Classic cocktails, great atmosphere. Pre-dinner drinks here every night.

places.name: Bass Harbor Head Lighthouse
places.note: 15 min from Southwest Harbor. One of the most photographed lighthouses on the East Coast — set dramatically into rocky shoreline. Go at golden hour. Arrive early to claim a spot on the rocks below.

places.name: Acadia Mountain Trail
places.note: Short (2.5 mi RT), steep, and the views of Somes Sound from the summit are outstanding. Perfect final hike before a relaxed dinner.

places.name: Beech Mountain + Fire Tower
places.note: Great moderate hike on the quiet western side of the island. Far fewer crowds than the Bar Harbor side. Fire tower at the top with 360° views of the island.

places.name: Botanica Spa at The Claremont
places.note: Book a massage before you arrive. After 3 days of Acadia hiking, your legs will demand it. Walk-ins are unlikely to work during Memorial Day weekend.

alerts.text: 🚗 Cadillac Summit Road requires a vehicle reservation via Recreation.gov (May 21–Oct 25). Opens 2 days prior at 10am EST. Set a reminder — or just hike up on foot. No reservation needed for hikers.
🥞 Late May = end of mud season. Expect wet/muddy trails especially at lower elevations. Waterproof hiking boots are not optional.
🏟️ Buy an America the Beautiful National Parks Pass ($80) before you go. Covers Acadia’s $35 entrance fee plus every other national park for a year.

📵 Cell service is limited in Bar Harbor and spotty throughout Acadia. Download offline maps before you leave. T-Mobile offers a free trial e-SIM — worth activating before the trip if you’re on a different carrier.

✈️ Two flights out of BGR on May 29. DL 5254 (Stacy/Justin/Ford) departs 12:15 PM. AA 1463 (Jeremy & Jennie) departs 2:22 PM. The Claremont is ~30 min from BGR. Stacy’s group needs to leave by 10:30am. Plan checkout accordingly.

items.text: Waterproof hiking boots (mandatory — mud season in late May)
Hiking poles (optional but helpful on Beehive)
Base layer + mid layer (mornings are cold)
Wool or synthetic hiking socks (x3 pairs)
America the Beautiful Pass ($80 — covers Acadia)
Wind-resistant fleece or jacket (it’s cold on the water)
Grippy boat shoes or old sneakers
Camera or GoPro with extra storage
One smart-casual outfit (Scales, Havana, Little Fern)
Dress pants or dark jeans (no hiking gear at dinner)
Light sport coat or blazer (optional, not required)
Tote bag (for market, fish market, souvenirs)
Ibuprofen or Advil (legs will be sore after Acadia)
Sunscreen (two bottles — you’ll go through it)
Download offline Acadia trail maps (AllTrails or Gaia GPS)
Screenshot or save recreation.gov reservation for Cadillac
America the Beautiful Pass (buy before you leave)
